title: Baissea urceolata
taxonomy: Baissea urceolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Baissea urceolata is native to tropical Africa and is often found in forests and woodland areas. The plant is known to have a woody vine habit and produces clusters of small, white, and fragrant flowers. Within the Apocynaceae family, Baissea urceolata is placed in the subfamily Rauvolfioideae.
uses: Baissea urceolata is a plant species native to tropical Africa, and research suggests it has potential medicinal properties. Baissea urceolata has been studied for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in treating various infections and inflammatory conditions.
